_comment: Steel grades. Steinmetz coefficients (kh, alpha, beta, ke) are calibrated for SI units: P_iron[W/m^3] = kh * f^alpha * B^beta + ke * f^2 * B^2 * t_lam_mm^2. kh has units W/m^3 / (Hz^alpha * T^beta), ke has units W/m^3 / (Hz^2 * T^2 * mm^2). Calibrated against typical W/lb published values (M19 ~5 W/lb at 60Hz/1.5T). Sources: AK Steel/Cleveland-Cliffs and Cogent (Tata) public datasheets; replace with vendor-specific values for serious work.

M19_29Ga.description: Non-oriented silicon steel, 0.014 in (0.35 mm) lamination, Class 29.

M15_29Ga.description: Higher-grade non-oriented silicon steel, lower core loss than M19.

Hiperco50.description: Cobalt-iron, very high saturation; for high-power-density designs.
